description: Next-state head learnability audit of the manufactured corpus (src/data/nextstate_audit.py): the complement to e-0005's categorical-head audit, targeting the world model's hardest head — next-state exact-match. CPU-only over all shards, it reports the copy baseline P(obs_after==obs_before), the (obs_before,action)->obs_after determinism / exact-match ceiling, per-Obs-field change rates (file/goals/messages/env), and the char/approx-token context budget of model input and target (tokens≈chars/4) to check single-T4/QLoRA feasibility.
motivation: e-0005 cleared the necessary precondition for the two categorical heads (status/error). The next-state exact-match head was unaudited. Before any GPU (e-0002) we can cheaply check the matching preconditions: is the transition deterministic (so exact-match isn't capped by label noise), is it non-trivial (copy baseline well below 1), and does the observation fit a T4 context?
formal_results: On the 66-record fixture corpus (6 decls, 3 shards): copy baseline 0.000 (no transition is a no-op echo); 58 distinct (obs_before,action) groups, 0 non-deterministic collisions -> exact-match ceiling 1.000. Field-change rates: file 0.970, goals 0.818, messages 0.788, env 0.000. Context budget: input mean 125c / p95 190c(48t) / max 256c(64t); target mean 127c / p95 311c(78t) / max 578c(145t). 118 tests pass (+10).
results_description: The next-state head clears its necessary precondition: the transition is a deterministic function in the data (ceiling 1.0, zero collisions) so exact-match is not capped by label noise, and it is non-trivial (copy baseline 0.0) so a copy-the-input model scores 0 — the head must genuinely predict. Dynamics concentrate in file/goals/messages; env never moves at fixture scale (the env sub-target is trivially copy here). The observation is tiny — p95 input ~48 tokens, max target ~145 — so a sub-2B model at a 2k QLoRA context on a T4 has enormous headroom.
conclusions: Together with e-0005 (positive action->target MI on the status/error heads), the learnability preconditions for q-0005 now hold across ALL of the world model's heads: categorical (status/error) AND next-state (exact-match deterministic + non-trivial + context-feasible). Necessary, not sufficient (the model must still read state; env-dynamics and length both grow at Mathlib scale and must be re-checked). Green light for e-0002 stands and is strengthened.
